mnemonics.title: Gram-Schmidt Steps
mnemonics.content:
Q:Question - Start with first vector
U:Use - Subtract projections
N:Normalize - Divide by norm
G:Get - Move to next vector
S:Subtract - All previous projections
C:Continue - Till all done
H:Handle - The remaining vectors

acronyms.expansion: Q = Orthogonal (Q columns orthonormal), R = Right (upper triangular matrix)
acronyms.usage: Remember: Q has orthonormal columns, R is right (upper) triangular
Two main steps in orthogonalization process

visualTips.visualization: Draw A as rectangle, Q as square with orthonormal arrows, R as triangular with non-zero entries above diagonal only
Think of projecting each vector onto the subspace spanned by previous orthogonal vectors, then subtracting to get perpendicular component
Imagine reflecting a vector across a plane (mirror) - the reflection plane is perpendicular to vector u
R has zeros below the main diagonal like a right-angled triangle in upper right corner

analogies.analogy: Like decomposing a force vector into perpendicular components - Q gives direction (unit vectors), R gives magnitude (how much of each)
Like creating a coordinate system from a set of directions - first direction is arbitrary, each subsequent direction must be made perpendicular to all previous
Like a rotation matrix - it rotates/reflections without stretching or squashing (preserves lengths and angles)

analogies.analization: Like reflecting light in a mirror - the mirror passes through origin, u is the normal to the mirror
